title: Introduction, Combinational Circuits and Boole Functions
main_topics: II. Combinational Circuits and Boole Functions
subtopics: II.3. Circuit Implementation Through Boole Functions
key_concepts: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, NXOR gates

definitions.description: An algebraic structure (B, +, ·, ¯) with two binary operations and one unary operation satisfying specific axioms including identity, constants, idempotence, complementarity, commutativity, associativity and distributivity.
A circuit whose output values depend only on the current input values; same input always yields the same output.
A circuit whose output depends on both the current input and the internal state.
A logical expression composed of an OR of AND terms, where each AND term contains all variables (possibly negated).
The dual of DNF: an AND of OR terms.
The speedup gained from improving a component of fraction fa that runs a times faster: A(a, fa) = 1 / ((1-fa) + fa/a)
If a memory location is accessed at a certain moment, it will very likely be accessed again in the near future.
If a memory location is accessed, its neighboring locations will very likely be accessed in the near future.
A compact way to define a Boole function by listing the row indices (minterms) in the truth table where the output is 1.

formulas.name: Number of distinct n-variable, m-output Boole functions

algorithms.name: Algebraic minimization of Boole functions
algorithms.description: Use Boole algebra laws (idempotence, unification) to simplify the DNF expression.

algorithms.name: Quine-McCluskey method
algorithms.description: Tabular method for minimizing Boole functions.

examples.name: Majority of 3 inputs
examples.description: f(x1,x2,x3) = Σ(3,5,6,7) – the function is 1 when more than half the inputs are 1.

examples.name: Odd parity function
examples.description: Returns 1 when an odd number of inputs are 1.

exercises: Prove that {NOT, AND}, {NOT, OR}, {NAND}, {NOR} are each sets of generators.
Find the DNF and study minimization for the 'odd' function.
learning_objectives: Understand the evolution of computing from abacus to von Neumann machines
Apply Amdahl's law to evaluate system improvements
Describe the difference between combinational and sequential circuits
Construct truth tables and derive Boole expressions
Apply Boole algebra axioms and theorems
Derive DNF and CNF from a truth table
Use sigma-notation to define Boole functions
Identify minimal sets of logic gate generators
Understand algebraic minimization of Boole functions
